q: A telescope is an optical instrument used for viewing nearby objects.
Astronomical telescope and terrestrial telescope are the two types of telescopes.
A refracting astronomical telescope consists of an objective of short focal length and an eyepiece of long focal length.
Both lenses in a refracting astronomical telescope are concave lenses.
Galileo invented a reflecting telescope.
In a refracting astronomical telescope, a real and inverted image of a distant object is formed at the focus of the eyepiece.
The final image formed by the eyepiece of an astronomical telescope is virtual, smaller than the first image and erect with respect to the distant object.
The inverted final image in an astronomical telescope causes problems for viewing planets, stars, and moons.
The magnifying power (MP) of the refracting astronomical telescope is defined as the ratio of the angle $\beta$ subtended by the object to the angle $\alpha$ subtended by the image.
For a refracting astronomical telescope, when the final image is formed at infinity, the separation of the lenses $L=f_o-f_e$.
A high magnifying power for the refracting astronomical telescope requires an objective of short focal length and an eyepiece of long focal length.
To obtain sharp images of distant objects with a telescope, the objective should have a small diameter.
A concave mirror instead of a convex lens is sometimes used as an objective in telescopes to avoid defects in large diameter convex lenses.
Most telescopes used at astronomical observatories in the world are refracting telescopes.
The reflecting telescope, called the Newtonian design, was first suggested by Galileo.
Astronomical telescopes are convenient for viewing objects on Earth since the final image produced is erect.
The astronomical telescope must be modified to produce a final image which is erect for viewing objects on Earth.
In the terrestrial telescope, a convex lens is used as an eyepiece to produce a final image which is erect.
Terrestrial telescopes of the basic type are very short and therefore convenient to use.
Total reflecting prisms (90°-45°-45° prisms) are used in binoculars to increase the length of the terrestrial telescope.
A binoculars is an optical instrument which consists of a single telescope.
Because of the successive total internal reflections in the prisms, the length of binoculars is greatly reduced.
Binoculars are widely used as astronomical telescopes.
